headline: Samsung & SK Hynix shares tumble 9%+ as chip rout spreads
detail: A 9%+ drop in the world's biggest memory chipmakers signals real sector stress, not just noise. This directly hits your SMH, SOXL, and any chip-adjacent names - when Asian chip giants crack hard, US semis typically follow at open. On margin, that cascade matters more than usual.
rec: Watch SMH closely at open - if it breaks toward $580-$590, consider trimming to reduce chip concentration before your $540 stop gets tested. SOXL stop at $200 must be honored if triggered - no exceptions on a 3X leveraged name in a rout.

headline: Apple plans five new iPhones through 2027, eyes Chinese-made chips
detail: Apple exploring Chinese-made chips is a longer-term signal that could pressure TSM's pricing power and US-centric chip suppliers. Near-term it's a watch item, not a panic - but it adds to the cautious chip narrative already in play today. For your book it's another reason to avoid adding chip exposure right now.
rec: No action today - the Chinese chip angle is a slow-burn risk, not an immediate trade. Keep it on radar if you're considering adding TSM (scanner entry $443.34-$443.79, stop $442.47) - wait for sector stabilization first.

headline: OpenAI proposes 5% stake to Trump administration
detail: If this deal gets traction it's a positive signal for AI infrastructure broadly - good for names like CRWD and the broader tech/cloud complex you're long. It also keeps AI capex narrative alive, which supports chip demand longer term. Short-term impact is muted but the sentiment is constructive.
rec: No action needed - CRWD is already your best performer at +7.4% with no scanner sell signal. This headline supports holding it, nothing more to do right now.

headline: M&A is 'on fire' as large-cap firms simplify, per Citi UK CEO
detail: An active M&A environment tends to be a tailwind for large-cap tech and cybersecurity names - CRWD, PANW are the kinds of assets that attract strategic interest. Doesn't move the needle today but supports the bull case for quality tech holdings. Your QQQ and SPY exposure also benefits from M&A-driven multiple expansion.
rec: No action - this is a slow macro tailwind, not a trigger. PANW does have a strong scanner setup (ACCUMULATE 9/12, entry $351.34-$351.69, stop $350.62) but wait for chip sector to stabilize before adding any new tech exposure today.
